a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: – Only those who both (a) have symptoms and also (b) meet specific criteria (e.g. key workers, admitted to
hospital, came into contact with a known case, returned from overseas).
– Testing of anyone showing COVID-19 symptoms.
– Open public testing (e.g. “drive through” testing available to asymptomatic people).
Data source: Blavatnik School of Government, University of Oxford (2023)
Note: Our data on COVID-19 tests and positive rate is no longer updated since 23 June 2022.
